21.correct: Bengal is known for its Baul singing tradition - a mystic, devotional folk-music form of wandering minstrels.

wrong.A: Gujarat is known for Garba, not Baul.
wrong.B: Tamil Nadu has Carnatic and folk forms, not Baul.
wrong.D: Punjab is known for Bhangra and Sufi music.

21.fact: Baul music of Bengal is recognised by UNESCO as an Intangible Cultural Heritage.

22.correct: In Hindustani music, Raga Yaman belongs to the Kalyan thaat.
22.fact: There are ten basic thaats (parent scales) in the Hindustani classical system.

23.correct: In a plant's vascular system, the vessels and tracheids (xylem elements) are responsible for conducting water.

wrong.A: Sieve tubes and companion cells (phloem) conduct food, not water.
wrong.C: Guard cells and stomata regulate gas exchange.
wrong.D: Phloem fibres provide support, not water conduction.

23.fact: Xylem carries water and minerals upward; phloem transports food made in the leaves.

24.correct: Decomposers (such as bacteria and fungi) are mainly responsible for breaking down the dead remains of plants and animals.

wrong.A: Herbivores eat plants; they are consumers, not decomposers.
wrong.B: Producers (green plants) make food, they do not decompose remains.
wrong.D: Carnivores eat other animals; they are consumers.

24.fact: Decomposers recycle nutrients back into the soil, completing the nutrient cycle.

25.correct: Dead animals are biodegradable waste, as they can be broken down by natural decomposers.
25.fact: Biodegradable waste can be composted; non-biodegradable waste persists in the environment.

26.correct: Stained-glass windows are a prominent feature of the design of the Sacred Heart (Sacre Coeur) Church in Bangalore.

wrong.A: A domed roof is not its defining feature.
wrong.C: Corinthian columns are not the highlighted feature.
wrong.D: A flat roof is not characteristic.

26.fact: Many colonial-era churches in India feature Gothic-style stained glass.

27.correct: The green colour of leaves is due to the pigment chlorophyll, which captures light for photosynthesis.

wrong.A: Guard cells control stomatal opening, not colour.

27.fact: Chlorophyll absorbs red and blue light and reflects green, giving leaves their colour.

28.correct: The leaf is called the 'food factory' of a plant because photosynthesis takes place there.

wrong.A: The flower is the reproductive part.
wrong.B: The stem supports the plant and transports materials.
wrong.C: The root absorbs water and anchors the plant.

28.fact: Leaves contain chloroplasts where light energy is converted into chemical energy (glucose).

29.correct: The brain and spinal cord are made mainly of nervous tissue, which transmits electrical impulses.
29.fact: The brain and spinal cord together form the central nervous system (CNS).

30.correct: In July 2025, Prime Minister Narendra Modi was conferred Brazil's highest civilian honour, the Grand Collar of the National Order of the Southern Cross.

wrong.A: Droupadi Murmu did not receive this honour.
wrong.B: Rahul Gandhi did not receive it.
wrong.D: S. Jaishankar did not receive it.

30.fact: Several countries have conferred their highest honours on PM Modi during state visits.

31.correct: Rajendra Vishwanath Arlekar was appointed Governor of Kerala in December 2024.

wrong.A: Vijay Kumar Singh was appointed to another role.
wrong.B: This option does not name the appointee.
wrong.C: Hari Babu Kambhampati was an earlier governor of another state.

31.fact: Governors are appointed by the President and serve as the constitutional head of a state.

32.correct: In males, the testes are the endocrine glands that secrete the hormone (testosterone) associated with the onset of puberty.

wrong.D: The thymus is involved in immunity.

32.fact: Testosterone drives male secondary sexual characteristics during puberty.

33.correct: On World Environment Day 2025, Acharya Prashant was honoured with the 'Most Impactful Environmentalist' award.

wrong.B: Gajendra Singh Shekhawat was not the awardee.
wrong.C: Narendra Modi was not the recipient.
wrong.D: Sonu Sood was not the recipient.

33.fact: World Environment Day is observed on 5 June every year.

34.correct: The Indian Institute of Space Science and Technology (IIST) is located in Thiruvananthapuram, Kerala.

wrong.C: It is not in New Delhi.

34.fact: IIST is Asia's first space university, set up under the Department of Space.

35.correct: Both Oxandrolone (I) and Oxabolone (II) are anabolic agents banned in sport, so 'Both I and II' is correct.

wrong.D: 'Neither' is wrong since both are anabolic agents.

35.fact: Anabolic agents are listed on the WADA Prohibited List.

36.correct: Among north-eastern states, Manipur won the most medals at the National Games 2025.

wrong.B: Arunachal Pradesh did not top among NE states.
wrong.C: Assam did not win the most among NE states.
wrong.D: Mizoram did not top the NE tally.

36.fact: Manipur is a sporting powerhouse, producing many national and international athletes.

37.correct: General K.M. Cariappa became the first Indian Commander-in-Chief of the Army in 1949 and was later made a Field Marshal.

wrong.A: P.N. Thapar was a later Army Chief.
wrong.C: K.S. Thimayya was a later Army Chief.
wrong.D: S.M. Shrinagesh was a later Army Chief.

37.fact: India has had only two Field Marshals: K.M. Cariappa and Sam Manekshaw.

38.correct: In the QS Asia University Rankings 2025, the University of Delhi was ranked around 81.
38.fact: The QS rankings assess universities on factors like academic reputation and research.

39.correct: India was ranked 18th in the Global Business Complexity Index 2025.
39.fact: A higher rank in this index means doing business is more complex in that jurisdiction.

40.correct: Sachin Tendulkar played his first Test match against Pakistan, in 1989 at Karachi.

wrong.A: His debut was not against Sri Lanka.

40.fact: Sachin made his Test debut at just 16, facing the pace of Wasim Akram and Waqar Younis.
